title: ELISA Protocol
author: ELISAassays
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.f6ebrbe
materials: After experiment, store all reagents according to the specified storage temperature respectively until their expiry.
Cover the plate with sealer we provided.
Gently tap the plate to ensure thorough mixing.

steps.title: Incubate for 120 minutes at 37 ⁰C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 120 minutes at 37 ⁰C. 02:00:00

steps.title: Invert the plate and pat it...
steps.instruction: Invert the plate and pat it against thick clean absorbent paper.

steps.title: Incubate for 60 minutes at 37°C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 60 minutes at 37°C. 01:00:00

steps.title: (wash 1/5) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 1/5) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: Add 50µL of Stop Solution to each well
steps.instruction: Add 50µL of Stop Solution to each well. If color change does not appear uniform, gently tap the plate to ensure thorough mixing.

steps.title: Determine the optical density (OD value)...
steps.instruction: Determine the optical density (OD value) of each well at once, using a microplatereader set to 450 nm. User should open the micro-plate reader in advance, preheat the instrument, and set the testing parameters.

steps.title: Add 100µL of Standard, Blank, or Sample per well
steps.instruction: Add 100µL of Standard, Blank, or Sample per well. The blank well is added with Sample diluent. Solutions are added to the bottom of micro ELISA plate well, avoid inside wall touching and foaming as possible.

steps.title: Mix it gently
steps.instruction: Mix it gently.

steps.title: Remove the liquid from each well, don't wash
steps.instruction: Remove the liquid from each well, don't wash.

steps.title: Add 100µL of Detection Reagent A...
steps.instruction: Add 100µL of Detection Reagent A working solution to each well.

steps.title: Incubate for 1 hour at 37°C. Note
steps.instruction: Incubate for 1 hour at 37°C. Note : if Detection Reagent A appears cloudy warm to room temperature until solution is uniform.

steps.title: (wash 1/3) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 1/3) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) (a squirt bottle, multi-channel pipette, manifold dispenser orautomated washer are needed). Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: After the last wash, completely remove...
steps.instruction: After the last wash, completely remove remaining Wash Buffer by aspirating or decanting.

steps.title: Add 100µL of Detection Reagent B...
steps.instruction: Add 100µL of Detection Reagent B working solution to each well.

steps.title: Add 90µL of Substrate Solution to each well
steps.instruction: Add 90µL of Substrate Solution to each well.

steps.title: Cover with a new Plate sealer...
steps.instruction: Cover with a new Plate sealer and incubatefor 15-30 minutes at 37°C. Protect the plate from light. The reaction time can be shortened orextended according to the actual color change, but this should not exceed more than 30 minutes.When apparent gradient appears in standard wells, user should terminate the reaction. 00:15:00

steps.title: (wash 2/3) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 2/3) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: (wash 3/3) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 3/3) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: (wash 2/5) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 2/5) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: (wash 3/5) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 3/5) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: (wash 4/5) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 4/5) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: (wash 5/5) Aspirate each well and...
steps.instruction: (wash 5/5) Aspirate each well and wash by filling each well with Wash Buffer (approximately 400µL) Complete removal of liquid at each step is essential.

steps.title: After the last wash, completely remove...
steps.instruction: After the last wash, completely remove remaining Wash Buffer by aspirating or decanting.

steps.title: Invert the plate and pat it...
steps.instruction: Invert the plate and pat it against thick clean absorbent paper.
